title: Type 2 diabetes review with medication adherence issues

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Your HbA1c has risen to 8.7%. How are you going with metformin and empagliflozin?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I take metformin most days but stopped empagliflozin last month because I was worried about thrush.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any genital symptoms now, urinary symptoms, dehydration, vomiting, abdominal pain, or ketone checks?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No symptoms now. I had thrush once. No vomiting or abdominal pain. I have not checked ketones.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Your blood pressure today is 148/88 and urine ACR is up. We should restart empagliflozin with sick-day advice, discuss thrush prevention, increase ramipril, and repeat renal function and potassium in two weeks.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: What should make me stop the tablet?

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Stop during vomiting, dehydration, fasting, or severe illness and seek urgent care for abdominal pain, vomiting, rapid breathing, or drowsiness.

candidate_notes.note: T2DM review. HbA1c 8.7%, BP 148/88, urine ACR increased. Taking metformin most days; stopped empagliflozin last month after thrush concern. No current GU symptoms, UTI symptoms, vomiting, dehydration, abdominal pain. Plan: restart empagliflozin with thrush prevention and SGLT2 sick-day advice; increase ramipril; repeat renal function/K in 2 weeks. Urgent advice for vomiting, abdominal pain, rapid breathing, drowsiness.
Diabetes control has worsened. Continue metformin and empagliflozin. Blood pressure elevated. Lifestyle advice given and repeat bloods planned.

S: HbA1c 8.7. Metformin taken most days. Empagliflozin stopped due to thrush concern. No current GU symptoms, vomiting, abdominal pain, dehydration. O: BP 148/88, ACR increased. A: T2DM above target, albuminuria, adherence issue. P: Restart empagliflozin, increase ramipril, repeat UEC/K in 2 weeks.

T2DM with HbA1c 8.7 and albuminuria. Empagliflozin stopped after thrush concern; no current GU/UTI symptoms or dehydration/vomiting/abdominal pain. Restart empagliflozin with thrush prevention and sick-day rules. Stop during vomiting, dehydration, fasting, or severe illness. Urgent review for abdominal pain, vomiting, rapid breathing, drowsiness. Increase ramipril and repeat renal function/K in 2 weeks.

CDSS diabetes/renal checklist: HbA1c 8.7, BP 148/88, ACR up. Medication reconciliation found empagliflozin stopped last month; metformin intermittent adherence. No current SGLT2 adverse symptoms. Plan: restart empagliflozin with sick-day and euglycaemic DKA red flags, address thrush prevention, increase ramipril, UEC/K in 2 weeks.
